exemplar.title: Should some knowledge not be sought on ethical grounds?

detailedExemplarData.title: Should some knowledge not be sought on ethical grounds?

strengths: Clearly identifies three specific realâworld objects and situates each in an appropriate context
Makes explicit links from each object to the chosen IA prompt, supported by concrete evidence and references
Presents a coherent overall narrative that moves beyond mere description to ethical evaluation
weaknesses: Analysis occasionally shifts into moral opinion rather than sustained TOK reflection on knowledge issues
Repetitive phrasing and imprecision in parts of the explanations (e.g., Trojanâhorse code privacy issues)
Underuses TOK terminology and explicit knowledge questions, limiting theoretical depth
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the exhibition demonstrates many strengthsâclear identification of objects, wellâsupported links to the IA prompt and a coherent narrativeâseveral limitations prevent a higher mark. The commentary sometimes drifts into moral opinion, lacks precise TOK terminology (for example, an explicit knowledge question), and contains repetitive or imprecise explanations in places. On balance, the work aligns with the midârange of the rubric (bands 5â6) but does not fully reach the depth or precision required for higher bands. Given the objective total mark of 5, this final criterion score fairly reflects both the submissionâs substantial strengths and its areas for development.

annotations.comment: The historical context of Milgramâs shock box is clearly presented, anchoring the object in a well-known experiment that examines obedience and moral conflict.
While criticisms of Milgram are noted, engage more deeply with the knowledge issue of deceptionâhow it influences the reliability and validity of the knowledge produced.
Incorporate reflection on participant emotions or ethical self-awareness as a way of knowing to enrich the TOK analysis of the experimentâs impact.
The link to the prompt notes value versus cost but omits a discussion of current ethical review standards; comparing Milgram with modern protocols would clarify evolving limits.
The analysis does not address informed consent, a central TOK concept in human sciences. Including this would strengthen the ethical evaluation of the experiment.
The final discussion balances psychological risks against research value, effectively illustrating the ethical tension central to the IA prompt.
To deepen the justification, compare Milgramâs methodology with contemporary ethical review processes, showing how acceptable boundaries for knowledge pursuit have evolved.
The exhibition would benefit from a concluding TOK insight that synthesizes all three objects to articulate a cohesive understanding of ethical limits on knowledge.
The exhibition begins with the precise IA prompt, clearly framing the exploration of ethical limits to knowledge and setting a focused TOK context for subsequent objects.

The student should reframe the prompt into an explicit knowledge question (e.g., âTo what extent should ethical considerations limit the pursuit of knowledge?â) to reinforce the deeper TOK inquiry at the exhibitionâs core.

The description of Clinique Black Honey lip gloss is vivid but leans heavily on personal routine. Specify its role in the cosmetics industry and regulatory context to strengthen its real-world grounding.
Integrate explicit details about Chinese regulatory requirements for cosmetics to clarify why Clinique conducts animal testing there, thereby deepening the objectâs contextual relevance.
The definition of animal testing remains descriptive and detached from TOK terminology. Link the methods used to ways of knowing and different perspectives to situate it within the TOK framework.
The student effectively considers stakeholders and proposes alternative testing methods, demonstrating a move from mere description to ethical evaluation aligned with TOK objectives.
The conclusion would benefit from invoking TOK concepts such as justification or bias when discussing ethical testing methods, adding theoretical depth to the argument.

The assertion that consumers must advocate for ethical testing lacks concrete examples of successful advocacy impacting corporate policy; incorporating a case study would strengthen the justification.
Consider citing a real-world example where consumer advocacy led to policy change, demonstrating how ethical knowledge-seeking can influence industry practices.
Selecting the Trojan Horse code compellingly highlights the trade-off between privacy invasion and child protection, making a strong link to the ethical prompt.
The narrative of Brad Willmanâs actions dominates the analysis; an explicit discussion of the knowledge question around digital surveillance as a way of knowing would deepen the TOK exploration.
Introduce how different areas of knowledge (e.g., ethics versus human sciences) interpret privacy to enrich the analysis with cross-AOK perspectives.
The claim about police threatening to arrest Willman would benefit from citation of a specific legal case or jurisdiction to substantiate its credibility.
Refine the justification for the Trojan Horse code to focus on its unique contribution to the ethical inquiry, avoiding repetition of earlier moral assertions.
Discuss how the reliability and validation of digital evidence differ from traditional forms of evidence to underscore a TOK-focused knowledge issue.
The conclusion effectively weighs privacy infringement against child safety, showing how the object links to the IA prompt through ethical balancing.
The statement could explore long-term knowledge implications of violating privacy normsâsuch as erosion of trustâto deepen critical analysis beyond immediate safety benefits.
Reference relevant ethical theories (e.g., utilitarianism, deontology) when evaluating privacy versus safety to situate the discussion within a broader TOK ethical framework.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
